response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack and systems engineer with an impressive career at Google and Meta, but he is a poor match for this C++-centric industrial automation role. His C++ experience is limited to a six-month engagement at National Instruments over a decade ago and a bachelor's thesis project — C++ does not even appear in his skills section. His primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/React, and he has no documented exposure to industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), IoT platforms, IEC 62443 secure development lifecycle practices, or on-premises automation systems, all of which are core to this position.
strengths: 15+ years of engineering experience including staff-level roles at Google and Meta, demonstrating sustained technical seniority
Hands-on event-driven and high-throughput systems work (DLang microservices processing millions of HTTP requests per second at Sociomantic)
Strong software craftsmanship foundation: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, pair programming, and a documented mentoring track record
concerns: C++ proficiency is negligible — one brief role at NI in 2014–2015 and a thesis-level project; not listed among his skills at all
No industrial automation, IoT, or embedded systems domain knowledge; no evidence of MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, IEC 61850, OPC UA, or DNP 3.0 experience
Zero exposure to secure development lifecycle (SDL) practices, IEC 62443, or industrial cybersecurity concepts required by the role

key_factors.factor: Senior engineering track record at top-tier companies
